Maybe the only thing that matters is if it feels good to me, but I'm scared it won't. I don't know if I can even start because I'm so anxious about what my friends will think.","problems_referred":["low_self_esteem","procrastination","conflicts_with_friends","loneliness"]}

parsed.simulated_user_message: I keep thinking that if I finish something, it will still feel pointless. Maybe the only thing that matters is if it feels good to me, but I'm scared it won't. I don't know if I can even start because I'm so anxious about what my friends will think.

parsed.reasoning: I'm here to support you without judgment, and I can see that you're struggling with feelings of pointlessness and anxiety about what others might think. Given your resistance, I want to acknowledge the fear that's holding you back and emphasize your autonomy in deciding what to create and why. The candidate strategies that seem most fitting are support and complex_reflection, as they allow us to explore your concerns without imposing any pressure. I've noticed that you've mentioned the watercolor of your old studio as a source of pride, and I wonder if we can use that as a reference point to understand what might make your current projects feel more meaningful. My entry point will be to reflect your concerns and acknowledge the difficulty of creating when faced with anxiety and self-doubt.

evidence_used.content: fear of being judged by own standards
watercolor of old studio as a source of pride

parsed.final_response: It's really scary to think that your creations might not feel meaningful, even if you finish them. The fear of what your friends will think is overwhelming, and it's making you question whether it's even worth starting. I want to acknowledge that it takes a lot of courage to admit these fears. Can you tell me more about what's driving your desire for your art to feel good to you, and what you think might happen if you were to create something just for yourself, without worrying about what others think?
